compiled_state.title: Write RED tests for ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Story DD Coverage
This task is responsible for satisfying the following story done definitions:
- DD4: The test suite includes a well-formed fixture case, a malformed fixture case, and an idempotency case

## TDD Requirement
Write failing bash tests for ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh before the script exists. Tests must fail (script not found) and be gate-tolerant via RED marker in .test-index. Three test functions required.

## Test Approach (Given / When / Then)

### test_migrate_file_impact_well_formed_fixture
- Given a tracker dir with a ticket containing a COMMENT event whose data.body has "## File Impact\n- src/foo.py (modified)\n- src/bar.py (deleted)"; When ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh runs on the tracker; Then a FILE_IMPACT event file is written in the ticket dir containing data.file_impact with [{path: "src/foo.py", reason: "modified"}, {path: "src/bar.py", reason: "deleted"}], and the stamp file .claude/.file-impact-migration-v1 exists at repo root.

### test_migrate_file_impact_malformed_fixture
- Given a tracker dir with one ticket whose COMMENT event body has "## File Impact\n(no parseable paths here — malformed)"; When ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh runs; Then the script exits 0 (not crashed), the malformed ticket ID is written to stderr, and the tracker is NOT corrupted (no FILE_IMPACT event for the malformed ticket).

### test_migrate_file_impact_idempotency
- Given a tracker dir that was already migrated (stamp file present); When ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh is run a second time on the same tracker; Then it exits 0 immediately, no new FILE_IMPACT events are written, and the stamp file still exists (no duplication).

## File Impact
- CREATE: tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh
- MODIFY: .test-index (add RED marker for ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh)

## Implementation Notes
- Follow tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh as the reference pattern
- Use `clone_ticket_repo` from tests/lib/git-fixtures.sh (creates repo with .tickets-tracker initialized)
- Use mktemp for temp dirs — never hardcoded /tmp paths
- Suite-runner guard: exit 0 early when script absent AND _RUN_ALL_ACTIVE=1 (same pattern as brainstorm test)
- Test file must be executable (chmod +x)
- Source REPO_ROOT/tests/lib/assert.sh and REPO_ROOT/tests/lib/git-fixtures.sh
- Stamp file path: tracker_root/../.claude/.file-impact-migration-v1 (same pattern as brainstorm uses .claude/.brainstorm-tag-migration-v2)
- Fuzzy-match: "ticketmigratefileimpactv1sh" IS a substring of "testticketmigratefileimpactv1sh" → compatible

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh exists
  Verify: test -f $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh
- [ ] File is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh
- [ ] File contains 3 test functions (well-formed, malformed, idempotency)
  Verify: grep -c "^test_migrate_file_impact_" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] Tests are RED before implementation (script-not-found exit or SKIP)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh 2>&1 | grep -E "SKIP|not found" | grep -q .
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker [test_migrate_file_impact_well_formed_fixture] for plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh
  Verify: grep "test_migrate_file_impact_well_formed_fixture" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
